title: Evidence Packaging Decision Guide
description: Apply (Bloom L3) evidence-packaging rules by selecting the correct packaging material for a given evidence item and seeing the forensic reason and courtroom risk of a wrong choice.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Evidence Packaging
Crime Scene Investigation
Evidence Preservation
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — select the correct packaging material for a given evidence item.
chapter_title: Crime Scene Investigation and Evidence Collection

learningObjectives: Select the correct packaging material for wet and dried biological evidence, dry trace, sharps, firearms, biohazards, digital devices, and impression casts.
Explain why breathable paper preserves biological evidence while airtight plastic causes mold growth and DNA degradation.
Connect each packaging choice to its courtroom consequence, including admissibility and handler safety.
educational.prerequisites: Concept of physical evidence types; basic idea of DNA and contamination; awareness of chain of custody

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Decision-practice tool: a randomized evidence case card requires the learner to select one of five packaging choices, then delivers immediate correct/incorrect feedback with the forensic reason and courtroom risk; a Reference mode allows free browsing of every category
pedagogical.assessment: Students choose packaging before any answer is shown; the sim tracks a running score and accuracy across attempts
pedagogical.modelType: Decision/classification model mapping evidence types to correct packaging with consequence feedback
